name: generate_radar_chart
description: Generate a radar chart to display multidimensional data (four dimensions or more), such as, evaluate Huawei and Apple phones in terms of five dimensions: ease of use, functionality, camera, benchmark scores, and battery life. Returns an interactive MCP-UI resource by default (format='html') that renders directly in compatible clients, a URL to an interactive HTML page (format='html-url'), or a static PNG image URL (format='png') for reports and documents.

data.description: Data for radar chart, it should be an array of objects, each object contains a `name` field and a `value` field, such as, [{ name: 'Design', value: 70 }].

backgroundColor.description: Background color of the chart, such as, '#fff'.

palette.description: Color palette for the chart, it is a collection of colors.

texture.description: Set the texture for the chart, optional, default is 'default'. 'rough' refers to hand-drawn style.

lineWidth.description: Line width for the lines of chart, such as 4.

style.description: Custom style configuration for the chart.

theme.description: Set the theme for the chart, optional, default is 'default'.

width.description: Set the width of chart, default is 600.

height.description: Set the height of chart, default is 400.

format.description: Output format: 'html' returns an interactive MCP-UI resource (default), 'html-url' returns a URL to an interactive HTML page, 'png' returns a static image URL. MCP-UI resources render directly in compatible clients for better interactivity.
